summary: Interstitial fluid volume is derived indirectly by measuring extracellular fluid volume—using radiolabeled water—and plasma volume—with radiolabeled albumin—then applying the formula: interstitial fluid volume = ECFF – PV. This method relies on tracer kinetics and known physiologic distributions to infer values without direct assessment.
